description: Audit existing C++/Qt codebase: catalog all major subsystems (PDF rendering, image rendering, CLI argument parsing, settings, headers/footers, TOC generation, JS evaluation, network handling) and document their interfaces and dependencies.

Research Rust ecosystem equivalents: identify crates for HTML/CSS rendering (e.g. servo/webrender), PDF generation (e.g. printpdf, lopdf), image output (e.g. image crate), headless browser automation (e.g. chromiumoxide, headless_chrome), CLI parsing (clap), HTTP client (reqwest), and JS execution.

Define Rust workspace layout: create a Cargo workspace with crates for `core` (rendering engine abstraction), `pdf` (PDF output), `image` (image output), `cli` (command-line interface), `settings` (configuration/settings structs), and `tests` (integration/regression tests).

Port settings and configuration system to Rust: implement all global, page-object, header/footer, margin, crop, TOC, and outline settings as strongly-typed Rust structs with serde support for config file loading.
Implement CLI argument parsing in Rust using clap: replicate all wkhtmltopdf and wkhtmltoimage command-line flags, options, and sub-commands with full help text.

Implement headless rendering abstraction layer in the `core` crate: define a trait-based renderer interface that accepts HTML input (URL or file) and produces a rendered page representation, with an initial implementation backed by a headless Chromium/WebKit engine.

Implement PDF output pipeline in the `pdf` crate: convert rendered pages to PDF with support for multi-page documents, page size/orientation, margins, DPI, compression, metadata (title/author/subject), and PDF/A compliance.
Implement image output pipeline in the `image` crate: support PNG, JPG, BMP, SVG, and other formats with configurable width, quality, crop settings, and DPI.
Implement header and footer rendering: support HTML-based headers/footers with template variable substitution ([page], [toPage], [date], [title], [url]), spacing, line options, and font settings.
Implement Table of Contents (TOC) generation: auto-generate TOC from heading elements with configurable depth, XSLT-based styling, dotted lines, and page number references.
Implement outline/bookmarks support: generate PDF bookmarks from document headings with configurable depth and nesting.
Implement JavaScript execution support: allow enabling/disabling JS, setting JS delay, and executing custom JS before rendering.
Implement network/proxy support: handle HTTP authentication, cookies (file and string), custom headers, SSL peer/host verification settings, and proxy configuration.
Implement C-compatible shared library API (libwkhtmltox replacement): expose a C ABI with the same function signatures as the original libwkhtmltox for drop-in compatibility with existing bindings.

Build regression test harness: create a test framework in the `tests` crate that renders a set of reference HTML fixtures with both the old wkhtmltopdf binary (if available) and the new Rust implementation, producing output files for comparison.

Create comprehensive HTML test fixtures covering: basic text/layout, tables, images, CSS (flexbox, grid, print media), page breaks, headers/footers, multi-page documents, TOC, JavaScript-rendered content, external resources, Unicode/RTL, and edge cases.

Implement visual diffing tool: integrate perceptual image diffing (e.g. using the `dssim` or `image` crate with pixel diff) to compare PDF-rendered-to-image output between reference and new implementation, reporting diff percentage and generating annotated diff images.
Implement PDF structural diffing: compare PDF metadata, page count, text content (via pdf-extract or similar), and outline structure between reference and Rust output beyond just visual comparison.
Write unit tests for all settings parsing, CLI argument handling, and configuration validation.
Write integration tests for PDF and image rendering pipelines covering all major feature flags and option combinations.

Update Cargo.toml dependencies to latest stable versions; audit for security advisories using `cargo audit`; pin MSRV (Minimum Supported Rust Version) compatible with Ubuntu 25's packaged Rust toolchain.
Add Ubuntu 25 (Plucky Piculet) build support: update packaging scripts, verify Qt/WebKit or Chromium headless library availability on Ubuntu 25, and document any required system dependencies.

Update GitHub Actions workflow for CI: create a matrix build across Ubuntu 22, Ubuntu 24, Ubuntu 25, macOS, and Windows; run `cargo build`, `cargo test`, clippy lints, and `cargo audit` on each platform.
Add GHA workflow for visual regression testing: run the regression test harness on PRs, store diff images as artifacts, and fail the build if any fixture exceeds a configurable visual diff threshold.
Add GHA workflow for release packaging: build release binaries for Linux (x86_64, aarch64), macOS, and Windows; produce .deb packages for Ubuntu 22/24/25; publish artifacts on tagged releases.

Update README.md and documentation: rewrite build instructions, document new Rust-based architecture, list system dependencies per OS, describe the visual regression test suite, and update the project status to active.
Port or replace the libwkhtmltox C API documentation (currently Doxygen) to Rust doc comments with `cargo doc`; generate and publish HTML docs via GitHub Pages.

Performance benchmarking: measure rendering throughput (pages/second) and memory usage for the Rust implementation vs the original C++ baseline on a standard fixture set; document results and optimize hot paths.
Security hardening: enable sandbox isolation for the headless renderer subprocess, audit all file path handling for path traversal, validate all network inputs, and add a security policy document.
Create CHANGELOG entry and version bump for the first Rust-based release; draft a GitHub release with migration notes for users of the old C++ binary and libwkhtmltox API.
